questions.question: Explain the three main components of Cost of Quality with one example for each.
keyPoints: Prevention costs - costs incurred to avoid defects (e.g., training, process improvement)
Appraisal costs - costs incurred to detect defects (e.g., testing, inspection)
Failure costs - costs from defects (internal failures during development, external failures after release)

questions.question: Distinguish between Quality Assurance (QA) and Quality Control (QC) with examples.
keyPoints: QA is proactive - prevents defects through process improvement (e.g., process definition, training)
QC is reactive - detects defects in the product (e.g., testing, code reviews)
QA focuses on processes, QC focuses on products

questions.question: Why should software quality be integrated from the beginning of project planning?
keyPoints: Prevents costly rework later in development lifecycle
Reduces defect detection and fixing costs
Ensures customer satisfaction and timely delivery
Avoids damaged reputation and business loss
Early defect detection is 10-100 times cheaper

questions.question: Describe the Five Maturity Levels of CMMI in ascending order.
keyPoints: Level 1: Initial - ad hoc, unpredictable processes
Level 2: Managed - process discipline, repeat previous successes
Level 3: Defined - processes are well characterized and understood
Level 4: Quantitatively Managed - measured and controlled
Level 5: Optimizing - continuous process improvement

questions.question: What is Six Sigma? Explain the DMAIC methodology.
keyPoints: Six Sigma is a data-driven methodology aiming for 3.4 DPMO
DMAIC stands for: Define, Measure, Analyze, Improve, Control
Define - identify problem and goals
Measure - collect baseline data
Analyze - find root causes
Improve - implement solutions
Control - sustain improvements

questions.question: List and briefly explain four important software quality metrics.
keyPoints: Defect Density - defects per thousand lines of code
Mean Time Between Failures (MTBF) - reliability measure
Test Coverage - percentage of code tested
Defect Detection Efficiency - defects found before release vs total
Maintainability Index - ease of modification

questions.question: Explain what is meant by 'The Place of Software Quality in Project Planning'. Why is it essential to treat quality as an integral part of planning rather than an afterthought? Provide examples to justify your answer.
keyPoints: Quality must be integrated from the initial planning phase
All project phases must have quality activities
Quality as afterthought leads to: costly rework, customer dissatisfaction, delayed deliveries, damaged reputation
Early defect detection costs exponentially less
Quality planning includes: defining standards, metrics, creating QA/QC plans
Investment in prevention reduces overall project cost
Examples: requirements reviews in planning, design reviews, peer code reviews

questions.question: Explain Total Quality Management (TQM) and discuss its key principles. How does TQM differ from traditional quality control approaches?
keyPoints: TQM is an organization-wide approach to quality
Key principles: customer focus, continuous improvement, employee involvement, process approach
Involves all employees in quality efforts
Focuses on prevention not just detection
Differs from traditional QC: proactive vs reactive, organization-wide vs limited to QC department
Emphasizes continuous improvement cycle
Integrates quality into all business processes

questions.question: What is ISO 9001:2015? Discuss its key principles and explain how certification benefits software organizations in terms of quality assurance.
keyPoints: ISO 9001:2015 is an international quality management standard
Key principles: customer focus, leadership, engagement of people, process approach, improvement, evidence-based decision, relationship management
PDCA (Plan-Do-Check-Act) cycle
Benefits: improved consistency, customer confidence, market access
Provides framework for systematic quality management
Requires documented processes and continuous improvement
Enhances credibility with stakeholders

questions.question: Discuss the concept of Cost of Quality (CoQ). How does the investment in prevention and appraisal activities help in reducing failure costs? Illustrate with examples.
keyPoints: Cost of Quality = Prevention + Appraisal + Failure costs
Prevention costs: training, process improvement, tools - prevent defects
Appraisal costs: testing, inspections - detect defects
Failure costs: internal (during development) + external (after release) - expensive
Early investment in prevention/appraisal reduces failure costs
Example: $1 in prevention saves $10 in appraisal and $100 in failure
Defects found in requirements cost 1/10th of design defects, 1/100th of post-release
Organizations save significantly by shifting left on defect detection
